children.heading: Actual production history yield
children.label: 1 Actual production history yield
children.text: The term “actual production history yield” means the weighted average of the actual production history for each insurable commodity or noninsurable commodity, as calculated under subchapter I or the noninsured crop disaster assistance program, respectively.

children.heading: Actual production on the farm
children.label: 2 Actual production on the farm
children.text: The term “actual production on the farm” means the sum of the value of all crops produced on the farm, as determined under subsection (b)(6)(B).

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an eligible producer on a farm that has at least 4 years of actual production history yields for an insurable commodity that are established other than pursuant to , the actual production history for the eligible producer without regard to any yields established under that section;section 1508(g)(4)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an eligible producer on a farm that has less than 4 years of actual production history yields for an insurable commodity, of which 1 or more were established pursuant to , the actual production history for the eligible producer as calculated without including the lowest of the yields established pursuant to ; andsection 1508(g)(4)(B) of this titlesection 1508(g)(4)(B) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: in all other cases, the actual production history of the eligible producer on a farm.

children.heading: Adjusted noninsured crop disaster assistance program yield
children.label: 4 Adjusted noninsured crop disaster assistance program yield
A
children.text: in the case of an eligible producer on a farm that has at least 4 years of production history under the noninsured crop disaster assistance program that are not replacement yields, the noninsured crop disaster assistance program yield without regard to any replacement yields;

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an eligible producer on a farm that has less than 4 years of production history under the noninsured crop disaster assistance program that are not replacement yields, the noninsured crop disaster assistance program yield as calculated without including the lowest of the replacement yields; and

children.label: C
children.text: in all other cases, the production history of the eligible producer on the farm under the noninsured crop disaster assistance program.

children.label: A
children.text: section 7912 or 7952 of this title;

children.label: B
children.text: section 1102 or 1301(6) of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 (, 8751(6)); or7 U.S.C. 8712

children.label: C
children.text: a successor section.

children.heading: Crop of economic significance
children.label: 6 Crop of economic significance
children.text: The term “crop of economic significance” shall have the uniform meaning given the term by the Secretary for purposes of subsections (b)(1)(B) and (g)(6).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “disaster county” means a county included in the geographic area covered by a qualifying natural disaster declaration.

children.label: i
children.text: a county contiguous to a county described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: any farm in which, during a calendar year the actual production on the farm is less than 50 percent of the normal production on the farm.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “eligible producer on a farm” means an individual or entity described in subparagraph (B) that, as determined by the Secretary, assumes the production and market risks associated with the agricultural production of crops or livestock.

children.label: i
children.text: a citizen of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: a resident alien;

children.label: iii
children.text: a partnership of citizens of the United States; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a corporation, limited liability corporation, or other farm organizational structure organized under State law.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “farm” means, in relation to an eligible producer on a farm, the sum of all crop acreage in all counties that is planted or intended to be planted for harvest for sale or on-farm livestock feeding (including native grassland intended for haying) by the eligible producer.

children.heading: Aquaculture
children.label: B Aquaculture
children.text: In the case of aquaculture, the term “farm” means, in relation to an eligible producer on a farm, all fish being produced in all counties that are intended to be harvested for sale by the eligible producer.

children.heading: Honey
children.label: C Honey
children.text: In the case of honey, the term “farm” means, in relation to an eligible producer on a farm, all bees and beehives in all counties that are intended to be harvested for a honey crop for sale by the eligible producer.

children.heading: Farm-raised fish
children.label: 10 Farm-raised fish
children.text: The term “farm-raised fish” means any aquatic species that is propagated and reared in a controlled environment.

children.heading: Insurable commodity
children.label: 11 Insurable commodity
children.text: The term “insurable commodity” means an agricultural commodity (excluding livestock) for which the producer on a farm is eligible to obtain a policy or plan of insurance under subchapter I.

children.label: A
children.text: cattle (including dairy cattle);

children.label: B
children.text: bison;

children.label: C
children.text: poultry;

children.label: D
children.text: sheep;

children.label: E
children.text: swine;

children.label: F
children.text: horses; and

children.label: G
children.text: other livestock, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Noninsurable commodity
children.label: 13 Noninsurable commodity
children.text: The term “noninsurable commodity” means a crop for which the eligible producers on a farm are eligible to obtain assistance under the noninsured crop assistance program.

children.heading: Noninsured crop assistance program
children.label: 14 Noninsured crop assistance program
children.text: The term “noninsured crop assistance program” means the program carried out under .section 7333 of this title

children.heading: Normal production on the farm
children.label: 15 Normal production on the farm
children.text: The term “normal production on the farm” means the sum of the expected revenue for all crops on the farm, as determined under subsection (b)(6)(A).

children.heading: Qualifying natural disaster declaration
children.label: 16 Qualifying natural disaster declaration
children.text: The term “qualifying natural disaster declaration” means a natural disaster declared by the Secretary for production losses under .section 1961(a) of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 17 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.label: 18 Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.text: The term “socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher” has the meaning given the term in section 2279(e) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: a State;

children.label: B
children.text: the District of Columbia;

children.label: C
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Trust Fund
children.label: 20 Trust Fund
children.text: The term “Trust Fund” means the Agricultural Disaster Relief Trust Fund established under .section 2497a of title 19

children.heading: United States
children.label: 21 United States
children.text: The term “United States” when used in a geographical sense, means all of the States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall use such sums as are necessary from the Trust Fund to make crop disaster assistance payments to eligible producers on farms in disaster counties that have incurred crop production losses or crop quality losses, or both, during the crop year.

children.heading: Crop loss
children.label: B Crop loss
children.text: To be eligible for crop loss assistance under this subsection, the actual production on the farm for at least 1 crop of economic significance shall be reduced by at least 10 percent due to disaster, adverse weather, or disaster-related conditions.

children.label: i
children.text: the disaster assistance program guarantee, as described in paragraph (3); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the total farm revenue for a farm, as described in paragraph (4).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The disaster assistance program guarantee for a crop used to calculate the payments for a farm under subparagraph (A)(i) may not be greater than 90 percent of the sum of the expected revenue, as described in paragraph (5) for each of the crops on a farm, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Exclusion of subsequently planted crops
children.label: C Exclusion of subsequently planted crops
i
children.text: is produced on land that is not eligible for a policy or plan of insurance under subchapter I or assistance under the noninsured crop assistance program; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is subsequently planted on the same land during the same crop year as the crop for which disaster assistance is provided under this subsection, except in areas in which double-cropping is a normal practice, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: a payment rate for the commodity that is equal to the price election for the commodity elected by the eligible producer;

children.label: II
children.text: the payment acres for the commodity that is equal to the number of acres planted, or prevented from being planted, to the commodity;

children.label: aa
children.text: the adjusted actual production history yield; or

children.label: bb
children.text: the counter-cyclical program payment yield for each crop; and

children.label: I
children.text: a payment rate for the commodity that is equal to 100 percent of the noninsured crop assistance program established price for the commodity;

children.label: II
children.text: the payment acres for the commodity that is equal to the number of acres planted, or prevented from being planted, to the commodity; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the adjusted noninsured crop assistance program yield; or

children.label: bb
children.text: the counter-cyclical program payment yield for each crop.

children.heading: Adjustment insurance guarantee
children.label: B Adjustment insurance guarantee
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), in the case of an insurable commodity for which a plan of insurance provides for an adjustment in the guarantee, such as in the case of prevented planting, the adjusted insurance guarantee shall be the basis for determining the disaster assistance program guarantee for the insurable commodity.

children.heading: Adjusted assistance level
children.label: C Adjusted assistance level
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), in the case of a noninsurable commodity for which the noninsured crop assistance program provides for an adjustment in the level of assistance, such as in the case of unharvested crops, the adjusted assistance level shall be the basis for determining the disaster assistance program guarantee for the noninsurable commodity.

children.heading: Equitable treatment for non-yield based policies
children.label: D Equitable treatment for non-yield based policies
children.text: The Secretary shall establish equitable treatment for non-yield based policies and plans of insurance, such as the Adjusted Gross Revenue Lite insurance program.

children.label: I
children.text: the actual production by crop on a farm for purposes of determining losses under subchapter I or the noninsured crop assistance program; and

children.label: II
children.text: subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), to the extent practicable, the national average market price received for the marketing year, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: 15 percent of amount of any direct payments made to the producer under sections 1103 and 1303 of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 [, 8753] or successor sections;17 U.S.C. 8713

children.label: iii
children.text: the total amount of any counter-cyclical payments made to the producer under sections 1104 and 1304 of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 [, 8754] or successor sections or of any average crop revenue election payments made to the producer under section 1105 of that Act [];117 U.S.C. 87147 U.S.C. 8715

children.label: iv
children.text: the total amount of any loan deficiency payments, marketing loan gains, and marketing certificate gains made to the producer under subtitles B and C of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 [ et seq., 8751 et seq.] or successor subtitles;17 U.S.C. 8731

children.label: v
children.text: the amount of payments for prevented planting on a farm;

children.label: vi
children.text: the amount of crop insurance indemnities received by an eligible producer on a farm for each crop on a farm;

children.label: vii
children.text: the amount of payments an eligible producer on a farm received under the noninsured crop assistance program for each crop on a farm; and

children.label: viii
children.text: the value of any other natural disaster assistance payments provided by the Federal Government to an eligible producer on a farm for each crop on a farm for the same loss for which the eligible producer is seeking assistance.

children.label: i
children.text: to reflect the average quality discounts applied to the local or regional market price of a crop or mechanically harvested forage due to a reduction in the intrinsic characteristics of the production resulting from adverse weather, as determined annually by the State office of the Farm Service Agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: to account for a crop the value of which is reduced due to excess moisture resulting from a disaster-related condition; and

children.label: iii
children.text: as the Secretary determines appropriate, to reflect regional variations in a manner consistent with the operation of the crop insurance program under subchapter I and the noninsured crop assistance program.

children.heading: Maximum amount for certain crops
children.label: C Maximum amount for certain crops
children.text: With respect to a crop for which an eligible producer on a farm receives assistance under the noninsured crop assistance program, the national average market price received during the marketing year shall be an amount not more than 100 percent of the price of the crop established under the noninsured crop assistance program.

children.label: I
children.text: the adjusted actual production history yield of the eligible producer on a farm; and

children.label: II
children.text: the counter-cyclical program payment yield;

children.label: ii
children.text: the acreage planted or prevented from being planted for each crop; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 100 percent of the price election for the commodity used to calculate an indemnity for an applicable policy of insurance if an indemnity is triggered; and

children.label: i
children.text: 100 percent of the adjusted noninsured crop assistance program yield;

children.label: ii
children.text: the acreage planted or prevented from being planted for each crop; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 100 percent of the noninsured crop assistance program price for each of the crops on a farm.

children.heading: Normal production on the farm
children.label: A Normal production on the farm
children.text: The normal production on the farm shall equal the sum of the expected revenue for each crop on a farm as determined under paragraph (5).

children.heading: Actual production on the farm
children.label: B Actual production on the farm
I
children.text: 100 percent of the price election for the commodity used to calculate an indemnity for an applicable policy of insurance if an indemnity is triggered; and

children.label: II
children.text: the quantity of the commodity produced on the farm, adjusted for quality losses; and

children.label: I
children.text: 100 percent of the noninsured crop assistance program established price for the commodity; and

children.label: II
children.text: the quantity of the commodity produced on the farm, adjusted for quality losses.

children.heading: Payments
children.label: 1 Payments
children.text: The Secretary shall make livestock indemnity payments to eligible producers on farms that have incurred livestock death losses in excess of the normal mortality due to adverse weather, as determined by the Secretary, during the calendar year, including losses due to hurricanes, floods, blizzards, disease, wildfires, extreme heat, and extreme cold.

children.heading: Payment rates
children.label: 2 Payment rates
children.text: Indemnity payments to an eligible producer on a farm under paragraph (1) shall be made at a rate of 75 percent of the market value of the applicable livestock on the day before the date of death of the livestock, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $80,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2012 and 2013.

children.label: I
children.text: owned;

children.label: II
children.text: leased;

children.label: III
children.text: purchased;

children.label: IV
children.text: entered into a contract to purchase;

children.label: V
children.text: is a contract grower; or

children.label: aa
children.text: the current production year; or

children.label: bb
children.text: subject to paragraph (3)(B)(ii), 1 or both of the 2 production years immediately preceding the current production year.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: ii Exclusion
children.text: The term “covered livestock” does not include livestock that were or would have been in a feedlot, on the beginning date of the qualifying drought or fire condition, as a part of the normal business operation of the eligible livestock producer, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Drought monitor
children.label: B Drought monitor
children.text: The term “drought monitor” means a system for classifying drought severity according to a range of abnormally dry to exceptional drought, as defined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: is an owner, cash or share lessee, or contract grower of covered livestock that provides the pastureland or grazing land, including cash-leased pastureland or grazing land, for the livestock;

children.label: II
children.text: provides the pastureland or grazing land for covered livestock, including cash-leased pastureland or grazing land that is physically located in a county affected by drought;

children.label: III
children.text: certifies grazing loss; and

children.label: IV
children.text: meets all other eligibility requirements established under this subsection.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: ii Exclusion
children.text: The term “eligible livestock producer” does not include an owner, cash or share lessee, or contract grower of livestock that rents or leases pastureland or grazing land owned by another person on a rate-of-gain basis.

children.heading: Normal carrying capacity
children.label: D Normal carrying capacity
children.text: The term “normal carrying capacity”, with respect to each type of grazing land or pastureland in a county, means the normal carrying capacity, as determined under paragraph (3)(D)(i), that would be expected from the grazing land or pastureland for livestock during the normal grazing period, in the absence of a drought or fire that diminishes the production of the grazing land or pastureland.

children.heading: Normal grazing period
children.label: E Normal grazing period
children.text: The term “normal grazing period”, with respect to a county, means the normal grazing period during the calendar year for the county, as determined under paragraph (3)(D)(i).

children.label: A
children.text: a drought condition, as described in paragraph (3); or

children.label: B
children.text: fire, as described in paragraph (4).

children.heading: Assistance for losses due to drought conditions
children.label: 3 Assistance for losses due to drought conditions
i
children.text: is native or improved pastureland with permanent vegetative cover; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is planted to a crop planted specifically for the purpose of providing grazing for covered livestock.

children.label: I
children.text: the monthly feed cost for all covered livestock owned or leased by the eligible livestock producer, as determined under subparagraph (C); or

children.label: II
children.text: the monthly feed cost calculated by using the normal carrying capacity of the eligible grazing land of the eligible livestock producer.

children.heading: Partial compensation
children.label: ii Partial compensation
children.text: In the case of an eligible livestock producer that sold or otherwise disposed of covered livestock due to drought conditions in 1 or both of the 2 production years immediately preceding the current production year, as determined by the Secretary, the payment rate shall be 80 percent of the payment rate otherwise calculated in accordance with clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: 30 days;

children.label: II
children.text: a payment quantity that is equal to the feed grain equivalent, as determined under clause (ii); and

children.label: III
children.text: a payment rate that is equal to the corn price per pound, as determined under clause (iii).

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of an adult beef cow, 15.7 pounds of corn per day; or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any other type of weight of livestock, an amount determined by the Secretary that represents the average number of pounds of corn per day necessary to feed the livestock.

children.label: aa
children.text: the national average corn price per bushel for the 12-month period immediately preceding March 1 of the year for which the disaster assistance is calculated; or

children.label: bb
children.text: the national average corn price per bushel for the 24-month period immediately preceding that March 1; by

children.heading: Normal grazing period and drought monitor intensity
children.label: D Normal grazing period and drought monitor intensity

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary shall determine the normal carrying capacity and normal grazing period for each type of grazing land or pastureland in the county served by the applicable committee.

children.heading: Changes
children.label: II Changes
children.text: No change to the normal carrying capacity or normal grazing period established for a county under subclause (I) shall be made unless the change is requested by the appropriate State and county Farm Service Agency committees.

children.heading: D2
children.label: I D2
children.text: An eligible livestock producer that owns or leases grazing land or pastureland that is physically located in a county that is rated by the U.S. Drought Monitor as having a D2 (severe drought) intensity in any area of the county for at least 8 consecutive weeks during the normal grazing period for the county, as determined by the Secretary, shall be eligible to receive assistance under this paragraph in an amount equal to 1 monthly payment using the monthly payment rate determined under subparagraph (B).

children.label: aa
children.text: in an amount equal to 2 monthly payments using the monthly payment rate determined under subparagraph (B); or

children.label: bb
children.text: if the county is rated as having a D3 (extreme drought) intensity in any area of the county for at least 4 weeks during the normal grazing period for the county, or is rated as having a D4 (exceptional drought) intensity in any area of the county at any time during the normal grazing period, in an amount equal to 3 monthly payments using the monthly payment rate determined under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Assistance for losses due to fire on public managed land
children.label: 4 Assistance for losses due to fire on public managed land
i
children.text: the grazing losses occur on rangeland that is managed by a Federal agency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the eligible livestock producer is prohibited by the Federal agency from grazing the normal permitted livestock on the managed rangeland due to a fire.

children.heading: Payment rate
children.label: B Payment rate
children.text: The payment rate for assistance under this paragraph shall be equal to 50 percent of the monthly feed cost for the total number of livestock covered by the Federal lease of the eligible livestock producer, as determined under paragraph (3)(C).

children.label: I
children.text: beginning on the date on which the Federal agency excludes the eligible livestock producer from using the managed rangeland for grazing; and

children.label: II
children.text: ending on the last day of the Federal lease of the eligible livestock producer.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: An eligible livestock producer may only receive assistance under this paragraph for losses that occur on not more than 180 days per year.

children.label: i
children.text: obtained a policy or plan of insurance under subchapter I for the grazing land incurring the losses for which assistance is being requested; or

children.label: ii
children.text: filed the required paperwork, and paid the administrative fee by the applicable State filing deadline, for the noninsured crop assistance program for the grazing land incurring the losses for which assistance is being requested.

children.heading: Waiver for socially disadvantaged, limited resource, or beginning farmer or rancher
children.label: B Waiver for socially disadvantaged, limited resource, or beginning farmer or rancher
i
children.text: waive subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide disaster assistance under this subsection at a level that the Secretary determines to be equitable and appropriate.

children.heading: Waiver for 2008 calendar year
children.label: C Waiver for 2008 calendar year
children.text: In the case of an eligible livestock producer that suffered losses on grazing land during the 2008 calendar year but does not meet the requirements of subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall waive subparagraph (A) if the eligible livestock producer pays a fee in an amount equal to the applicable noninsured crop assistance program fee or catastrophic risk protection plan fee required under subparagraph (A) to the Secretary not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of this subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide equitable relief to an eligible livestock producer that is otherwise ineligible or unintentionally fails to meet the requirements of subparagraph (A) for the grazing land incurring the loss on a case-by-case basis, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: 2008 calendar year
children.label: ii 2008 calendar year
children.text: In the case of an eligible livestock producer that suffered losses on grazing land during the 2008 calendar year, the Secretary shall take special consideration to provide equitable relief in cases in which the eligible livestock producer failed to meet the requirements of subparagraph (A) due to the enactment of this subchapter after the closing date of sales periods for crop insurance under subchapter I and the noninsured crop assistance program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An eligible livestock producer may elect to receive assistance for grazing or pasture feed losses due to drought conditions under paragraph (3) or fire under paragraph (4), but not both for the same loss, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Relationship to supplemental revenue assistance
children.label: B Relationship to supplemental revenue assistance
children.text: An eligible livestock producer that receives assistance under this subsection may not also receive assistance for losses to crops on the same land with the same intended use under subsection (b).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 7 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $400,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2012 and 2013.

children.heading: Emergency assistance for livestock, honey bees, and farm-raised fish
children.label: e Emergency assistance for livestock, honey bees, and farm-raised fish

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide emergency relief to eligible producers of livestock, honey bees, and farm-raised fish to aid in the reduction of losses due to disease, adverse weather, or other conditions, such as blizzards and wildfires, as determined by the Secretary, that are not covered under subsection (b), (c), or (d).

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 2 Use of funds
children.text: Funds made available under this subsection shall be used to reduce losses caused by feed or water shortages, disease, or other factors as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: 3 Availability of funds
children.text: Any funds made available under this subsection shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2012 and 2013.

children.heading: Eligible orchardist
children.label: A Eligible orchardist
children.text: The term “eligible orchardist” means a person that produces annual crops from trees for commercial purposes.

children.heading: Natural disaster
children.label: B Natural disaster
children.text: The term “natural disaster” means plant disease, insect infestation, drought, fire, freeze, flood, earthquake, lightning, or other occurrence, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Nursery tree grower
children.label: C Nursery tree grower
children.text: The term “nursery tree grower” means a person who produces nursery, ornamental, fruit, nut, or Christmas trees for commercial sale, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Tree
children.label: D Tree
children.text: The term “tree” includes a tree, bush, and vine.

children.label: i
children.text: under paragraph (3) to eligible orchardists and nursery tree growers that planted trees for commercial purposes but lost the trees as a result of a natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: under paragraph (3)(B) to eligible orchardists and nursery tree growers that have a production history for commercial purposes on planted or existing trees but lost the trees as a result of a natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: An eligible orchardist or nursery tree grower shall qualify for assistance under subparagraph (A) only if the tree mortality of the eligible orchardist or nursery tree grower, as a result of damaging weather or related condition, exceeds 15 percent (adjusted for normal mortality).

children.label: i
children.text: reimbursement of 70 percent of the cost of replanting trees lost due to a natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary, in excess of 15 percent mortality (adjusted for normal mortality); or

children.label: ii
children.text: at the option of the Secretary, sufficient seedlings to reestablish a stand; and

children.label: B
children.text: reimbursement of 50 percent of the cost of pruning, removal, and other costs incurred by an eligible orchardist or nursery tree grower to salvage existing trees or, in the case of tree mortality, to prepare the land to replant trees as a result of damage or tree mortality due to a natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary, in excess of 15 percent damage or mortality (adjusted for normal tree damage and mortality).

children.heading: Definitions of legal entity and person
children.label: A Definitions of legal entity and person
children.text: In this paragraph, the terms “legal entity” and “person” have the meaning given those terms in section 1001(a) of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( (as amended by section 1603 of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008).7 U.S.C. 1308(a)3So in original. Probably should be followed by a second closing parenthesis.3

children.heading: Amount
children.label: B Amount
children.text: The total amount of payments received, directly or indirectly, by a person or legal entity (excluding a joint venture or general partnership) under this subsection may not exceed $100,000 for any crop year, or an equivalent value in tree seedlings.

children.heading: Acres
children.label: C Acres
children.text: The total quantity of acres planted to trees or tree seedlings for which a person or legal entity shall be entitled to receive payments under this subsection may not exceed 500 acres.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 5 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $20,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2012 and 2013.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of each insurable commodity of the eligible producers on the farm, excluding grazing land, did not obtain a policy or plan of insurance under subchapter I (excluding a crop insurance pilot program under that subchapter); or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of each noninsurable commodity of the eligible producers on the farm, did not file the required paperwork, and pay the administrative fee by the applicable State filing deadline, for the noninsured crop assistance program.

children.heading: Minimum
children.label: 2 Minimum
children.text: To be considered to have obtained insurance under paragraph (1)(A), an eligible producer on a farm shall have obtained a policy or plan of insurance with not less than 50 percent yield coverage at 55 percent of the insurable price for each crop planted or intended to be planted for harvest on a whole farm.

children.heading: Waiver for socially disadvantaged, limited resource, or beginning farmer or rancher
children.label: 3 Waiver for socially disadvantaged, limited resource, or beginning farmer or rancher
A
children.text: waive paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: provide disaster assistance under this section at a level that the Secretary determines to be equitable and appropriate.

children.heading: 2008 crop year
children.label: A 2008 crop year
children.text: In the case of an eligible producer that suffered losses in an insurable commodity or noninsurable commodity during the 2008 crop year but does not meet the requirements of paragraph (1), the Secretary shall waive paragraph (1) if the eligible producer pays a fee in an amount equal to the applicable noninsured crop assistance program fee or catastrophic risk protection plan fee required under paragraph (1) to the Secretary not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of this subchapter.

children.heading: 2009 crop year
children.label: B 2009 crop year
children.text: In the case of an insurable commodity or noninsurable commodity for the 2009 crop year that does not meet the requirements of paragraph (1) and the relevant crop insurance program sales closing date or noninsured crop assistance program fee payment date was prior to , the Secretary shall waive paragraph (1) if the eligible producer of the insurable commodity or noninsurable commodity pays a fee in an amount equal to the applicable noninsured crop assistance program fee or catastrophic risk protection plan fee required under paragraph (1) to the Secretary not later than 90 days after .August 14, 2008October 13, 2008

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide equitable relief to eligible producers on a farm that are otherwise ineligible or unintentionally fail to meet the requirements of paragraph (1) for 1 or more crops on a farm on a case-by-case basis, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: 2008 crop year
children.label: B 2008 crop year
children.text: In the case of eligible producers on a farm that suffered losses in an insurable commodity or noninsurable commodity during the 2008 crop year, the Secretary shall take special consideration to provide equitable relief in cases in which the eligible producers failed to meet the requirements of paragraph (1) due to the enactment of this subchapter after the closing date of sales periods for crop insurance under subchapter I and the noninsured crop assistance program.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a portion of the total acreage of a farm of the eligible producer that is not of economic significance on the farm, as established by the Secretary; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a crop for which the administrative fee required for the purchase of noninsured crop disaster assistance coverage exceeds 10 percent of the value of that coverage.

children.heading: Treatment of acreage
children.label: B Treatment of acreage
children.text: The Secretary shall not consider the value of any crop exempted under subparagraph (A) in calculating the supplemental revenue assistance program guarantee under subsection (b)(3) and the total farm revenue under subsection (b)(4).

children.label: i
children.text: pay the appropriate fee described in paragraph (4)(A) not later than 90 days after ; andFebruary 17, 2009

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of each insurable commodity of the eligible producers on the farm, excluding grazing land, agree to obtain a policy or plan of insurance under subchapter I (excluding a crop insurance pilot program under that subchapter) for the next insurance year for which crop insurance is available to the eligible producers on the farm at a level of coverage equal to 70 percent or more of the recorded or appraised average yield indemnified at 100 percent of the expected market price, or an equivalent coverage; and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of each noninsurable commodity of the eligible producers on the farm, agree to file the required paperwork, and pay the administrative fee by the applicable State filing deadline, for the noninsured crop assistance program for the next year for which a policy is available.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of each insurable commodity of the eligible producers on the farm, had obtained a policy or plan of insurance for the 2008 crop year at a level of coverage not to exceed 70 percent or more of the recorded or appraised average yield indemnified at 100 percent of the expected market price, or an equivalent coverage; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of each noninsurable commodity of the eligible producers on the farm, had filed the required paperwork, and paid the administrative fee by the applicable State filing deadline, for the noninsured crop assistance program for the 2008 crop year, except that in determining the level of coverage, the Secretary shall use 70 percent of the applicable yield.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount that would have been calculated under subparagraph (B) if the eligible producers on the farm had paid the appropriate fee under that subparagraph; or

children.label: I
children.text: in clause (i) of that subparagraph, “120 percent” is substituted for “115 percent”; and

children.label: II
children.text: in clause (ii) of that subparagraph, “125” is substituted for “120 percent”.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: D Limitation
children.text: For amounts made available under this paragraph, the Secretary may make such adjustments as are necessary to ensure that no producer receives a payment under this paragraph for an amount in excess of the assistance received by a similarly situated producer that had purchased the same or higher level of crop insurance prior to .February 17, 2009

children.heading: Authority of the Secretary
children.label: E Authority of the Secretary
children.text: The Secretary may provide such additional assistance as the Secretary considers appropriate to provide equitable treatment for eligible producers on a farm that suffered production losses in the 2008 crop year that result in multiyear production losses, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: suffered a production loss or multiyear production losses due to a natural cause during the 2008 crop year; and

children.label: aa
children.text: except as provided in item (bb), lack access to a policy or plan of insurance under subchapter I; or

children.label: bb
children.text: do not qualify for a written agreement because 1 or more farming practices, which the Secretary has determined are good farming practices, of the eligible producers on the farm differ significantly from the farming practices used by producers of the same crop in other regions of the United States; and

children.label: II
children.text: are not eligible for the noninsured crop disaster assistance program established by .section 7333 of this title

children.heading: Definitions of legal entity and person
children.label: 1 Definitions of legal entity and person
children.text: In this subsection, the terms “legal entity” and “person” have the meaning given those terms in section 1001(a) of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( (as amended by section 1603 of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008).7 U.S.C. 1308(a)3

children.heading: Amount
children.label: 2 Amount
children.text: The total amount of disaster assistance payments received, directly or indirectly, by a person or legal entity (excluding a joint venture or general partnership) under this section (excluding payments received under subsection (f)) may not exceed $100,000 for any crop year.

children.heading: AGI limitation
children.label: 3 AGI limitation
children.text: Section 1001D of the Food Security Act of 1985 () or any successor provision shall apply with respect to assistance provided under this section.7 U.S.C. 1308–3a

children.heading: Direct attribution
children.label: 4 Direct attribution
children.text: Subsections (e) and (f) of section 1001 of the Food Security Act of 1985 () or any successor provisions relating to direct attribution shall apply with respect to assistance provided under this section.7 U.S.C. 1308

children.heading: Transition rule
children.label: 5 Transition rule
children.text: Sections 1001, 1001A, 1001B, and 1001D of the Food Security Act of 1985 [, 1308–1, 1308–2, and 1308–3a] as in effect on , shall continue to apply with respect to 2008 crops.7 U.S.C. 1308September 30, 2007

children.heading: Period of effectiveness
children.label: i Period of effectiveness
children.text: This section shall be effective only for losses that are incurred as the result of a disaster, adverse weather, or other environmental condition that occurs on or before , or, in the case of subsections (c) through (f), as determined by the Secretary.September 30, 2011September 30, 20134So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.4

children.heading: No duplicative payments
children.label: j No duplicative payments
children.text: In implementing any other program which makes disaster assistance payments (except for indemnities made under subchapter I and ), the Secretary shall prevent duplicative payments with respect to the same loss for which a person receives a payment under subsections (b), (c), (d), (e), or (f).section 7333 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2) and notwithstanding any provision of subchapter I, subchapter I shall not apply to this subchapter.

children.heading: Cross references
children.label: 2 Cross references
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to a specific reference in this subchapter to a provision of subchapter I.
